hook: A Snapple drink line from 1999 with elemental names like Rain, Fire, and Sun, sold in wide-mouth glass bottles that fans kept long after the drinks were gone.
fate: Snapple Elements launched in 1999 and faded from most stores in the early 2000s. The line is no longer part of Snapple's regular lineup, but it is still one of the most talked-about discontinued drinks from that era.
summary: Snapple Elements was a drink line that Snapple launched in 1999. The drinks had elemental names like Rain, Sun, Fire, and Earth and came in wide-mouth glass bottles that looked different from other drinks on the shelf. The line grew through 2000 but faded from stores in the early 2000s. It is no longer made, but a lot of people still remember it and ask for it to come back.

seo.meta_title: What Happened to Snapple Elements? The Wide-Mouth Bottles People Still Miss
seo.meta_description: Snapple Elements launched in 1999 with unusual flavors and distinctive wide-mouth bottles. Here is what happened to Rain, Sun, Fire, and the rest of the line.

notable_products: Rain, a berry-flavored drink that is the most requested flavor in nostalgia conversations about the line
Sun, a citrus-based drink from the original 1999 lineup
Fire, a fruit drink with a warmer, spiced flavor profile
Earth, the fourth original flavor in the elemental lineup
verified_facts: The original line is widely remembered for elemental flavor names such as Rain, Sun, Fire, and Earth.
By 2000, trade coverage described the Elements line as having expanded beyond the original four drinks.
Snapple's own FAQ says some flavors are discontinued or sold only as limited-time offers.
Snapple Elements is no longer part of the core modern Snapple lineup.
cultural_memory: The wide-mouth glass bottles are almost as memorable as the drinks themselves.
Many people kept the bottles and reused them long after the drinks were gone.
Rain is often the most requested flavor in nostalgia discussions, with Fire and Sun close behind.
For many late-1990s and early-2000s fans, Snapple Elements represents a more experimental era of beverage branding.
overview_md:
**Snapple Elements** was a drink line that Snapple launched in 1999. The drinks had elemental names and came in wide-mouth glass bottles that looked different from anything else on the shelf at the time.

The original four flavors were Rain, Sun, Fire, and Earth. Each one had a fruit-based recipe and a name meant to match the feeling of the flavor. Rain was berry-forward. Sun leaned citrus. The bottles were short and wide with a big opening, which made them feel more like something you would keep around than throw away. A lot of people did keep them and used them for years after the drinks were gone.

Snapple launched the line in spring 1999. Trade coverage from 2000 showed the line had already grown beyond the original four drinks, with new flavors added to stores. The line was doing well enough that Snapple kept adding to it.

But like a lot of beverage side lines from that era, Snapple Elements did not last. The early 2000s brought more competition and changing tastes. Snapple had also been through several ownership changes in the years before and after the launch, which made it harder to keep a niche line going. By the mid-2000s the drinks were hard to find, and eventually they stopped being made.

Snapple Elements never got a proper comeback. Snapple has brought back other things over the years, but not this line. It comes up often in discussions about drinks people wish would return, and Rain in particular has a following that has held on for decades.

timeline.text: Snapple Elements launches with an elemental theme and distinctive wide-mouth glass bottles.
The line becomes strongly associated with bottles that fans later keep and reuse.
Trade coverage describes the line as having grown beyond the original four fruit drinks.
The original Snapple Elements line fades from regular U.S. retail availability.
Snapple Elements remains one of the brand's most requested discontinued lines in nostalgia discussions.

stats.modern_status_note: Original line discontinued; exact final end year is unclear in public sources

faq.question: When did Snapple Elements come out?
What flavors were in Snapple Elements?
faq.answer: The original line is most commonly remembered for Rain, Sun, Fire, and Earth, though later trade coverage shows the line expanded beyond those initial drinks.

faq.question: When was Snapple Elements discontinued?
faq.answer: The line faded from stores in the early-to-mid 2000s. The exact final year is not well documented in public sources.

faq.question: Why do people remember Snapple Elements so strongly?
faq.answer: Mostly because of the wide-mouth bottles, the elemental names, and flavors that felt different from the regular Snapple lineup.

faq.question: Is Snapple Elements still sold today?
faq.answer: No. The original line is gone and has not been brought back as a regular product.
